question: The word 'embedded' means ________.
choices: placed or fixed deeply inside something else

question: In the IoT infographic, a smartwatch is listed as an example of a ________ device that can measure activity and heart rate.
Read: 'Within a decade or so almost every appliance in your house could be connected.' Which passive tense is 'could be connected'?
Read: 'It has even been referred to as the fourth Industrial Revolution.' Which passive tense is used?
Read: 'Maher realised he had been followed.' The agent is not mentioned because it is ________.
Read: 'It will show people the consequences of an unhealthy lifestyle.' Rewrite in the passive focusing on PEOPLE: ________.
choices: The consequences will be shown to people
People will be shown the consequences
Consequences are shown to the people
The lifestyle will be shown to people

question: According to the grammar reference, which of the following verbs can also have two objects like 'show'?
choices: hand, offer, pay, promise, send, teach

question: In the speaking box, which phrase is used to SUMMARISE data at the end of a trend description?
choices: In this graph, we can see …
Overall, the trend in … is upwards/downwards
There has been a sharp increase in …

question: Read: 'Overall, there has been a steady rise in the number of IoT devices globally, but the technology has grown more rapidly in some areas than others.' The word 'steady' describes growth that is ________.
In the article 'Early Adopter or Laggard?', what does the article say early adopters are essentially paying for?
The word 'state-of-the-art' means ________.
The word 'glitches' are described as ________.
choices: small problems that prevent a device from working well

question: Read from the VR article: 'People with autism may have a hypersensitivity to sights, sounds, smells and tastes. Some people are able to cope with this kind of sensory overload reasonably well, though the stress might eat away at them inside.' 'Sensory overload' means ________.
choices: a physical injury caused by loud sounds
being overwhelmed by too many sensory stimuli at once

question: Read from the VR article: 'People can watch the video using special 3D glasses, which take them more fully into the experience.' The 3D glasses are used to ________.
choices: protect the eyes from the screen
allow people to interact with virtual objects
make the VR experience more immersive

question: The phrasal verb 'go through' means ________.
choices: to experience something, especially something difficult

question: The phrasal verb 'calm down' means ________.
choices: to focus on the present moment

question: Read from the VR article comprehension: 'The writer cites Chris Milk to illustrate that VR ________.'
choices: can be a way to communicate over distance
video may actually separate us from people we see onscreen
has the potential to alter how we interact with each other
creates perfect simulations of real life

question: Read from the VR article comprehension: 'The examples in paragraph 4 indicate that VR can ________.'
choices: easily trick people into doing things
inspire people to visit new places
stimulate people's willingness to take action

question: The word 'attributes' in the context of describing a person means ________.
choices: physical or personal characteristics or qualities

question: The word 'superficial' means ________.
choices: related only to the surface or appearance

question: Rewrite using Pattern (a): 'Some people have suggested that young people should not have mobile phones.' → It ________ by some people that young people should not have mobile phones.
Read from the Bell article: 'Although he is most famous for inventing the telephone, he also invented a device that could be used to ________.'
Read from the Bell article: 'Bell also invented an early ________ and a device to detect icebergs in the sea.'
In the essay writing box, 'Another positive aspect of … is …' is a phrase used to ________.
choices: give arguments in favour of the situation

question: Read: 'In spite of these potential benefits, a significant number of people are choosing to use ad-blocking software.' What is the function of 'In spite of' here?
choices: It adds more information to support benefits
It introduces a contrasting idea despite the benefits

question: Read from 'Our Personal Data': 'In some cases, this is because they find the adverts annoying but it is also because of a desire to avoid being tracked online.' Why do many people use ad-blocking software?
choices: They find adverts annoying and want to avoid being tracked

question: According to the Life Skills section on social media, social media can play an important part in your ________.
According to the Life Skills section on social media, you can never ________ something that has been on the internet.
According to the Life Skills section, if you follow someone on social media, make sure they are influencing you in a ________ way.
Choose the correct passive form: 'I regret ________ to buy this phone.' (persuade)
Choose the correct passive: 'Young people don't need ________ how to use new devices.' (tell)
Choose the correct passive: 'By 2035, a chip like this ________ in your wrist.' (could/embed)
The passive gerund is formed with ________.
Read: 'I would really like to be employed by one of the giant tech companies.' This sentence uses ________.
Read from the VR article: 'While we can experience the sounds and sights of a flood, we don't come up against the same difficulties.' This means that VR ________.
choices: cannot replicate the full difficulty of real situations
is better than real experience for learning
creates false impressions of real problems

question: In the Life Skills section on managing your online image, Tip 5 'Check your privacy settings regularly' is matched with extra information: ________.
choices: Remember that posting privately doesn't necessarily protect you
Ask yourself if your grandmother would be comfortable reading it
Set up an alert to let you know when someone mentions you online
Don't share anything belonging to someone else without permission

question: Read: 'It is commonly believed that education is being revolutionised by MOOCs, as the vast majority are free and qualifications are not required to register for them.' What does this sentence tell us about MOOCs?
choices: They are expensive and require qualifications
They are mostly free and open to anyone
They are only available in certain countries
They are replacing all traditional education

question: In the for-and-against essay ACTIVE WRITING section, it says a good essay needs to introduce different perspectives and critically ________ them.
Read: 'This is clearly far more convenient, even though it is, of course, less secure.' The phrase 'even though' is a ________.
According to the Life Skills section on managing your online image, employers today are very likely to look out for people with an ________ online presence.
